description: Consolidation decisions for widget_similarity.json clusters. decidedBy 'owner+claude' entries are taste decisions; execution status lives in codex_worklog.md.

decisions.rationale: Three ad-hoc label/value stat treatments; CatchStatColumn already covers all with monoValue flag. Standardizes stat typography (RunningStat flips from label-first kicker to value-first).
Title+subtitle section headers; CatchSectionHeader gained an optional subtitle slot. Call sites pass padding: EdgeInsets.zero to stay pixel-faithful.
Form-field wrapper (label + detail + child slot), not a stat; coarse fingerprint conflated it. Revisit against labelled.dart/CatchFormFieldLabel in a form-field pass.

Both re-implement Tooltip+CatchIconButton. Call sites pass size: CatchIconButton.defaultSize (canonical defaults navSize) and foregroundColor. Future candidate: rename CatchTopBarIconAction -> CatchIconAction since it is now used off top bars.

members.name: HostManageMetaItem
members.note: color: maps to icon tint; label defaults ink2

members.name: StageSectionLabel
members.note: icon section title, not meta; candidate for CatchSectionHeader icon variant later

decisions.rationale: One standard icon+label meta treatment (sm icon, labelM ink2, single line) added to catch_meta_row.dart beside CatchMetaDotRow.

members.name: CatchEventThumbnailScrimOverlay
members.note: already a parameterized core primitive tied to thumbnail styles; may delegate to CatchScrim later

decisions.rationale: Four gradient recipes on one skeleton; centralizing them makes the photo-treatment language a design-system asset and standardizes IgnorePointer wrapping. WO-016 added CatchOpacity.photoFrameEdge so the photo-frame preset no longer borrows the Event Success border token.
All six are thin Center(child: CatchEmptyState(...)) content bindings around the existing primitive. Not components; catalogue noise. Inline at single call sites and delete.

Already consolidated internally: both delegate to shared _CatchErrorSpec + CatchErrorBody. Two named entry points (full-screen vs inline) are intentional API ergonomics; merging is churn without UI improvement.

members.name: CatchPersonRosterLayout
members.note: fix raw `size: 11` -> CatchIcon.micro (token drift vs chat layout)

decisions.rationale: Intentional presentation variants over shared CatchPersonRowData (chat preview with unread emphasis vs roster meta). Merging with flags would be a conditional mess. Real finding is the raw icon size literal.
Byte-identical fractional bar-fill across host/user analytics. Note the trend: MetricTile (0.73) and panel pairs suggest a full host/user analytics-kit dedupe pass later.
Byte-identical 'Who's going' skeleton duplicated across two files of the same feature.

Five compositions of the same 'surface + N (leading shape, text lines) rows [+ title]' pattern across three features. One core primitive with a leading-shape enum; standardized text-width jitter (exact per-row widths are skeleton noise). WO-016 added divided-row support and absorbed the two previously skipped host wrappers.
Both are 'row of N equal Expanded skeleton boxes with gap' (quick actions, tab pickers). Generic enough for the catch_skeleton.dart convenience family.

members.name: GenderFilterSkeleton
members.note: height param stays: chips it stands in for are taller

decisions.rationale: Same jittered chip-wrap skeleton with drifted heights (s8 vs s9) and meaningless width jitter differences; standardize jitter, parameterize height.

Same titled-surface + text-block + trailing-boxes skeleton, both event_success. Stays feature-level (single-feature composition). Codex must diff bodies below the shared prefix and escalate if not isomorphic.

ChatShareCardSheet hand-rolls capture/grabber/share-button that CatchShareCardSheet already provides (club/event share flows use it in production). Parameter mapping is one-to-one; chat keeps its card widget.

decisions.clusterId: group/rotation family (c016, c028, c029, c030, c033, c044, c052 + VisibleGroupRotationSlots pairs)
decisions.rationale: Domain fork, not UI drift: micro-pods vs guided-rotations carry parallel typed rounds (EventSuccessGroupOverrideRound vs RotationOverrideRound) and genuinely different business logic (opt-out semantics, intervals, fairness totals). Generic unification would trade duplication for conditional complexity. Both compose tokenized primitives already. Rule of three: revisit if a third assignment mode appears.

members.name: CatchTabDockIcon
members.note: rewrite to delegate its badge overlay to CatchCountBadge

decisions.rationale: CatchTabDockIcon copy-pastes the entire count-badge overlay (same layout tokens, same 99+ logic). Promote the overlay as CatchCountBadge(count, child); tab dock icon becomes a thin composition.

members.name: SavedEventsMessage
members.note: inline; unify overrides to CalendarMessage's set (fixes eventInfoTileExtent icon-size token misuse)

decisions.rationale: Thin CatchEmptyState wrappers (same policy as c009). CalendarMessage/SavedEventsMessage additionally drifted on typography overrides; standardize both surfaces on the calendar treatment. Recon task: count the CatchScreenBody+Center(CatchEmptyState) pattern repo-wide — if >=4, next batch designs a CatchEmptyState screen variant.

members.name: PhotoSlotMainBadge
members.note: into existing CatchBadge via uppercase + ink/inverse styling

decisions.rationale: Different concepts sharing a skeleton: MapPill is a translucent mono overlay pill for imagery (keep; fix raw alpha literal), PhotoSlotMainBadge is a status badge CatchBadge already covers.

members.name: CatchConfirmDialog
members.note: rewrite internals to delegate shell to CatchFormDialog if shells are copy-pasted

decisions.rationale: Two entry points (confirm vs form) are good API ergonomics, same as the error-state pair. The overlay shell (Dialog + CatchSurface + title + actions row) must be shared internally, not duplicated — verify and delegate.
Identical PreferredSizeWidget shells (48-height padded CatchOptionGroup) differing only in option enum. Generic core primitive; tokenizes the raw 48 as CatchLayout.tabRailHeight.

members.name: HostProfileMissingState
members.note: keeps its ListView wrapper at call site

decisions.rationale: Same 'surface + sectionTitle + supporting body + CTA actions' empty card three times in hosts. Feature-level for now; promote to core if WO-008 recon shows the pattern is cross-feature.
Six-line shared shell around CatchInlineErrorState with clear intent-carrying names; merge value below churn threshold.

Per-tab skeleton compositions; parameterizing would move the same jitter to call sites. Real finding: raw title-width literals (148/190/170/150) instead of CatchLayout.skeletonText* tokens — drift fix in WO-013.
Composition relationships, not duplication: one member instantiates the other (screen wraps its body/content via builder). Fingerprint similarity is pass-through plumbing. Rule K1.

Same file, identical bodies except the media: argument (polaroid artwork vs photo overlay). Single widget; if call sites pick the variant from photo availability, fold that choice inside (mirroring ClubShareArtwork), else keep a media param.

members.name: PaperTicketSerial
members.note: rewrite build to compute strings then return PaperTicketDetail (accepts value maxLines 1->2)

decisions.rationale: Serial is Detail with computed content; delegate internally, both APIs stay.
Deliberate dual art direction (celebration cream overlay vs paper surface) on the known celebration-screen debt file; parameterizing four visual knobs for a 20-line loop is churn.
Typed domain rows in an internal policy lab screen; generics over preview-row models is complexity without user-facing value. Rule K2 (domain fork) on a lab surface.

members.name: LabHero
members.note: delegates shell; keeps its outer Padding/Center/ConstrainedBox

decisions.rationale: The accent->ink diagonal gradient hero recipe (gradient + transparent border + contentRelaxed) is copy-pasted across three event_success screens. Extract the shell as a shared feature widget; each hero keeps its content.

members.name: ProfileInlineMultiChoiceEntryEditor
members.note: deferred — generic classes, need type-aware read

members.name: ExploreRailLabel
members.note: deferred — possible overlap with CatchOptionGroup item rendering

decisions.rationale: Deferred, not decided: classes are generic (class X<T>) and need a proper read in the next batch. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-04): type-aware read done — editors stay distinct (see c010); the ExploreRailLabel/CatchOptionGroupItem overlap is real and resolved as an absorb (see c014, WO-021).

members.name: UnsavedChangesPill
members.note: warning-dot status indicator, distinct concept and tokens

decisions.rationale: Two content variants of the same CTA leading in the same file; parameterize icon+label. Not promoted to core: CTA-leading is an event-detail concept with 2 uses.

Rulebook scope limit: current cluster is scope: screen, so it needs review instead of mechanical merge. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): per-surface skeleton compositions; keep, but opportunistically migrate onto CatchSkeletonRows/BoxRow/Chips when files are next touched.

Rulebook scope limit: current cluster is scope: screen, so it needs review instead of mechanical merge. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): distinct product surfaces; structural similarity reflects the uniform screen architecture (CatchScreenBody/section stacks), which is the goal, not debt.

Rulebook scope limit: current cluster is scope: screen, so it needs review instead of mechanical merge. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): cross-concept structural coincidence; different design intents (per K5 read of names/roles against prior batch context).

Rulebook scope limit: current cluster is scope: screen, so it needs review instead of mechanical merge. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): loading/skeleton counterpart of the same surface; the pairing is intentional, not duplication.

Rulebook scope limit: current cluster is scope: screen, so it needs review instead of mechanical merge. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): per-surface skeleton compositions; keep, but opportunistically migrate onto CatchSkeletonRows/BoxRow/Chips when files are next touched.

Rulebook scope limit: current cluster is scope: screen, so it needs review instead of mechanical merge. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): loading/skeleton counterpart of the same surface; the pairing is intentional, not duplication.

Rulebook scope limit: current cluster is scope: screen, so it needs review instead of mechanical merge. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): deferred to next review batch — needs read. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-04): not screen composition after read — each is an R2-shape thin wrapper (CatchBottomDock(CatchButton(save))) over a typed footer state with exactly one call site. Inline the expression at each call site verbatim (keep the key and padding args) and delete both classes; the footer-state types stay.

Current-registry id for the prior c006 owner decision: StageSectionLabel is an icon section title, not an icon+label meta row; folding it into CatchMetaRow would cross section-title and meta-row concepts.
Current-registry id for the prior small-widget owner decision: UnsavedChangesPill is a warning-dot status indicator with distinct tokens, not an event-detail CTA leading icon+label.

K5 territory: both share the Semantics/InkWell/DecoratedBox/ConstrainedBox shell, but ActivityTypeRow's body is ActivityDot + titleL label + titleL count with selected semantics while MoreActivityTypesRow's body is kicker '+ n MORE TYPES' + forward arrow expand affordance. REVIEW ANSWER (claude, 2026-07-03): keep-distinct — divergent semantics would need ~5 knobs to reproduce; churn over value (K4/K5).

Current-registry id for prior c005-layout decision: chat preview with unread emphasis and roster meta are intentional presentation variants over CatchPersonRowData; the only drift was the raw roster icon size fixed in WO-005.

Current-registry id for prior c006-dialog/WO-010 decision: confirm and form dialogs remain separate public entry points; full-body inspection rejected direct delegation because action layout and content-slot contracts differ.
Current-registry id for the WO-006 skipped members. WO-016 added CatchSkeletonRows.divided and absorbed both divider-separated host wrappers into the shared primitive.
K2 domain fork: micro-pods and guided rotations carry parallel typed business logic and already compose tokenized primitives; generic unification would add conditional complexity.
K2 domain fork: group rotation slots and rotation slots bind to different assignment families with different semantics; keep the public widgets split until a third assignment mode appears.
K2 domain fork: group override and rotation override sheets are backed by parallel override model families and different business rules, so a generic sheet would concentrate conditional logic.
K2 domain fork: the round editors operate on EventSuccessGroupOverrideRound versus RotationOverrideRound and differ in opt-out/fairness/interval behavior, not just row chrome.
Current-registry id for prior c021-tab-skeleton/WO-013 decision: per-tab skeleton compositions stay distinct; the actionable drift was raw title-width literals, already tokenized in WO-013.
K2 domain fork from the prior event-policy rows decision: typed cancellation/result row groups live in an internal policy lab and generics would add complexity without user-facing value.
K2 domain fork from the prior event-policy rows decision: cancellation and result preview rows bind to different policy preview models and should not be unified by generic conditionals.
Current-registry id for prior c045-hero/WO-014 decision: the shared gradient hero shell was extracted as EventSuccessHeroSurface and the public heroes now delegate while keeping their content APIs.
K1 composition relationship from prior c046/c043/c057 decision: dashboard home variants are screen composition, not duplicate primitives.
K4 churn threshold from prior c061-companion-error decision: six-line shared shell around CatchInlineErrorState is below the threshold, and the names carry distinct intent.
K2 domain fork from the group/rotation family decision: group and reveal rotation slots carry different assignment semantics even though their row chrome is similar.
K4 churn threshold from prior c041-celebration-details-card decision: the cards intentionally carry different art direction, and parameterizing the visual knobs is churn.
K1 composition relationship from prior c046/c043/c057 decision: DashboardFull wraps DashboardFullSliverBody, so the similarity is pass-through composition rather than duplication.
K2 domain fork from the group/rotation family decision: micro-pod and rotation schedule cards expose different assignment models and business logic.
K1 composition relationship from prior c046/c043/c057 decision: ProfileTab instantiates ProfileTabContent, so the match is wrapper/content composition.
K2 domain fork from the group/rotation family decision: member and pair editors are backed by different override model families and should not be unified by conditional generics.

No exact K/R match: both are profile inline choice editors, but single-choice owns nullable selected state and optional empty-selection behavior while multi-choice owns Set<T> state, optional/non-optional removal rules, and patchForLatestProfile handling. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): deferred to next review batch — generic classes; needs type-aware read. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-04): type-aware read done. The editors already share every rendered layer (CatchField.actions shell, ProfileChipOptions/ProfileChipPlaceholder/ProfileChipValue, InlineSaveState mixin); the residue is genuinely forked selection logic (T? vs Set<T>, allowEmptySelection vs isOptional min-1 removal, different no-op equality checks, multi-only patchForLatestProfile). K3 plus genuine K2 — a merge would be a mode switch over two state machines.

K5 concept mismatch: EventSharePill is a light share-card metadata pill using primarySoft/labelS/compactLabelContent; EventSuccessDarkPill is a dark editorial overlay pill using editorialLight alpha/labelL/compactControlContent. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): light vs dark tone recipes; deliberate surface-specific variants.

No exact K/R match: ExploreRailLabel is structurally close to core CatchOptionGroupItem, but absorbing it requires a core option-item API decision around selectedRule, variant, option typing, and whether explore filter rails are option groups. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): deferred to next review batch — generic classes; needs type-aware read. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-04): ExploreRailLabel is a near-byte clone of CatchOptionGroupItem's label variant (same Semantics/InkWell/AnimatedContainer underline, micro10/s3 padding flip, micro3 rule, labelL ink/ink3) — the explore time rail IS an option-group row that only needed a scrollable layout the group shell doesn't provide. Core API loosened: selectedRule becomes optional (defaults to t.ink), variant defaults to label; explore constructs items directly in its scroll row. Accepted standardization: rail labels gain ellipsis overflow (visual noop at these lengths).

K5 concept mismatch: both render circular icon medallions, but CatchErrorIcon is the core danger/error medallion with configurable extent/iconSize while PaperCelebrationIcon is celebration-specific primary art direction with fixed 64/30 sizing. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): cross-concept structural coincidence; different design intents (per K5 read of names/roles against prior batch context).

K4 churn threshold: the mergeable shell is a small UserAnalyticsSection + CatchSurface + Column loop, but a shared panel would need label, row data, row builder, and separator policy knobs for two intent-carrying analytics concepts.

K5 concept mismatch: both are expandable disclosure shells, but CatchFrameworkErrorDebugDetails is core developer-error detail UI with danger label and debug text while SetupDisclosureSection is Event Success setup content with title, subtitle, and children. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): cross-concept structural coincidence; different design intents (per K5 read of names/roles against prior batch context).

K4 churn threshold: both use the same Event Detail surface/title/line spacing shell, but unifying them would require title, data derivation, conditional policy lines, and item-builder knobs for distinct overview concepts.

No exact K/R match: StructureNumberField was already kept as a form-field wrapper, while RevealHostCopy is event-success copy content; the shared skeleton does not identify one canonical component. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): cross-concept structural coincidence; different design intents (per K5 read of names/roles against prior batch context).

K5 concept mismatch: the shared icon/text/surface skeleton spans companion beats, live host cards, setup notices, and user analytics rows with different typography, token roles, and data contracts. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): low-cohesion chained cluster (union-find noise); member pairs individually triaged elsewhere.

K4 churn threshold: the common code is the sliver padding/adapter/center/constrained shell, while the children are insights, skeleton, and profile content flows that would need child, spacing, and state knobs.

K5 concept mismatch: these icon/text rows and cards carry setup, dashboard, paper-companion, profile, safety, and swipe-window semantics with different label/body/kicker and surface roles. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): low-cohesion chained cluster (union-find noise); member pairs individually triaged elsewhere.

K5 concept mismatch: all three are child surfaces, but StagePanel is the animated ambient stage card, StageActionDock is the dark action surface, and StageSoftBand is the soft nested band with different token roles. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): three distinct stage furniture roles (dock/panel/band) within one art direction; cohesion 0.47 is skeleton coincidence.
K4 churn threshold: the overlap is generic skeleton surface/column/row scaffolding, but a shared widget would need chart, host summary, club host, and browse-grid knobs for distinct loading concepts.

K4 churn threshold: both are event-detail CTA cards in one file, but unifying them would require event/share versus companion/open behavior, title/body, and button mapping knobs for two intent-carrying concepts.
K4 churn threshold: the shared skeleton atoms are small row/column fragments, while reproducing all members would require count, axis, padding, row content, and feature-shell knobs.

K2 domain fork: the public app shell owns public navigation/guest auth affordances while HostAppShell owns host navigation; unifying would trade duplication for host/public conditional business logic.

K4 churn threshold: both render skeleton section surfaces, but the event-success primitive owns title/text/trailing counts while the profile surface skeleton owns profile-line count and surface padding.

No exact K/R match: the shared CatchSelectChip/Wrap shape hides different multi-select, questionnaire, reveal countdown, and rotation-cadence APIs that need a feature-level chip-group design decision. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): deferred to next review batch — chips-row family; needs read against CatchSelectChip/option-group vocabulary. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-04): the titled chip-wrap is a layout convention, not one concept — typography intentionally differs by level (labelM setup control, labelL block title, supporting sub-field). Merge only the same-file twins RotationCadenceChips + RevealCountdownChips (identical labelM + gapH6 + Wrap bodies over const single-select option lists) into one feature-local generic SetupChoiceChips<T> taking List<CatchOption<T>>; option lists and label formatting move to call sites as data. QuestionnaireBlock keeps (3-state mode derivation + nested config editor); ActivityAttributeGoalChips keeps (multi-select contains-semantics, sub-field typography). No core primitive — a Catch* chip row would need a typography knob per concept (K5).
K4 churn threshold: both use badge/wrap people tokens, but VisiblePodAssignment wraps assignment-state copy and shelling while PeopleTokenRow is a compact count/loading/profile token row.
K4 churn threshold: the shared StagePanel/StageSectionLabel shell is small, and merging would need icon, label, title, body, and optional supporting-text knobs for two live-stage concepts.
K2 domain fork: EventFocusRail is member dashboard event-focus navigation while HostEventToolsCarousel is host management tooling with different data and action contracts.

R2 thin wrapper: ExploreEmptyState only wrapped CatchEmptyState in Center/Padding with fixed Explore copy variants and optional actions; production and Widgetbook uses were inlined with the same icon/copy/action values.

K2 domain fork: the tiles share the same card skeleton, but they render parallel HostAnalyticsMetricCard/UserAnalyticsMetricCard data, status enums, status-copy rules, and user analytics copy helpers. AUDIT (claude, 2026-07-03): K2 misapplied — bodies are structurally parallel presentation over parallel view-model types (same surfaces, tokens, layout; only typed field access/metric keys differ). Re-opened for the host/user analytics-kit unification review. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): identical layout/tokens over identical model shapes; kit takes display-ready CatchMetricCardData, features keep icon/format/copy mapping.

No exact K/R match: the bodies are near-identical host picker tiles, but they live in separate create/edit event surfaces and merging requires a shared host picker-tile contract, file ownership decision, and empty-string behavior standardization. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): identical params AND bodies across two host screens; one shared hosts widget. Later form-controls pass may fold into CatchField.nav.

K2 domain fork: the trend panels share chart chrome and CatchAnalyticsBar, but they read parallel HostAnalyticsTrendPoint/UserAnalyticsTrendPoint metrics with different metric keys, section shells, and summary stat copy. AUDIT (claude, 2026-07-03): K2 misapplied — bodies are structurally parallel presentation over parallel view-model types (same surfaces, tokens, layout; only typed field access/metric keys differ). Re-opened for the host/user analytics-kit unification review. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): aggregation + chart composition are feature semantics; both already share CatchAnalyticsBar and will share CatchAnalyticsSection. Remaining duplication dissolves with kit v1.

K2 domain fork: the grid skeleton is shared, but the members render parallel metric-card domains and tile types; the user grid also caps at six cards while the host grid renders the supplied host summary cards. AUDIT (claude, 2026-07-03): K2 misapplied — bodies are structurally parallel presentation over parallel view-model types (same surfaces, tokens, layout; only typed field access/metric keys differ). Re-opened for the host/user analytics-kit unification review. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): identical 2-col Wrap grid; user .take(6) becomes maxItems param.

K5 concept mismatch: both are dark pill shells, but DarkPill is a manual-QA accent-ink pill while EventSuccessDarkPill is a reused Event Success editorial overlay primitive with an optional foreground override and more than ten external usages. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): same dark pill concept; manual-QA screen mimics the feature surface and must reuse its pill. Reveal tokens win; manualQaPill* opacity tokens become orphan candidates.

K4 churn threshold: both headers share title/subtitle/trailing structure, but branded sheets own a glyph tile, tighter subtitle gap, and smaller subtitle type while plain sheets are text-first; merging would add variant knobs without reducing meaningful duplication.

K5 concept mismatch: CatchMetaRow is icon plus single-line metadata using labelM/ink2, while EventPolicyLabSectionTitle is a section-title row with md icon, sectionTitle typography, and optional trailing controls. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): cross-concept structural coincidence; different design intents (per K5 read of names/roles against prior batch context).

No exact K/R match: OverlayIconAction is close to CatchTopBarIconAction, but preserving it requires a reviewed API decision for semantic wrapper ownership, floating-control background alpha, button extent, and row-sized icon support. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): OverlayIconAction is the third tooltip+CatchIconButton wrapper; canonical params already cover its floating treatment. Also triggers the planned rename CatchTopBarIconAction -> CatchIconAction (used off top bars since slice 1). Icon size standardizes row -> md.

No exact K/R match: the optimistic body is a route-level fallback composition with fixed stubs and no callbacks, while EventDetailBody owns the full loaded state/action contract; merging needs an event-detail state API decision. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): optimistic pre-data render of the same surface; intentional counterpart pair.

K5 concept mismatch: CatchSectionLabel is a core kicker/header label with optional icon and bounded-width behavior, while StageSectionLabel is an Event Success live-stage row using labelL typography, required color, and two-line text. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): StageSectionLabel belongs to the companion stage/paper art direction (celebration-cards precedent); CatchSectionLabel already covers the kicker+icon case for standard surfaces.

K2 domain fork: both are photo strips, but the club strip renders actual club photos and count copy while the event strip owns event photos, fixed placeholder slots, and activity-colored fallback art.

No exact K/R match: CatchVeiledPersonAvatar could become a named avatar state, but absorbing it requires a reviewed core avatar API decision for veiled activity placeholders and border behavior. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): same-file complementary variants (real person vs privacy-veiled placeholder with different data needs).

K5 concept mismatch: CatchSectionHeader is a section title/subtitle/trailing primitive, while HostOrganizerSectionHeader is a mono-label row with optional neutral text action and host-organizer semantics. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): CatchSectionHeader already covers it via titleStyle (monoLabel ink2) + trailing (CatchTextButton).

K2 domain fork: both are detail hero sliver app bars, but club and event heroes own different domain models, action sets, collapsed title treatments, media-height calculations, and presentation modes.
K1 composition is not duplication: HostOperationsTopBar is a host-specific PreferredSizeWidget adapter that constructs CatchTopBar with host title/search/filter/action state.
K4 churn threshold: both live in the ordered photo picker and share a raised tile shell, but one is an add-photo action while the other is an image tile with remove/reorder/cover affordances.

K5 concept mismatch: the event-detail summary is attendee-facing booking policy copy inside a detail surface, while the policy-lab summary is a scenario/debug policy-shape panel with divider rows and lab-only fields. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): user-facing event summary vs policy-lab scenario summary; lab tooling.

K4 churn threshold: the existing WO-009 recon found CatchCountPill owns a floating control with caller-supplied badge text, while CatchPersonUnreadCountPill owns chat unread semantics, 99+ clamping, and CatchBadge styling.

K5 concept mismatch: CatchEmptyStateContent is the core empty-state layout body, while EmptyHeroContent is the dashboard editorial hero CTA with welcome eyebrow, marketing copy, and light button treatment. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): EmptyHeroContent is bespoke dashboard editorial hero copy, not generic empty-state content.
K1 composition is not duplication: EventDetailSocialSection composes the Who's Going section and conditionally instantiates EventReviewsSection when member context is available.

K5 concept mismatch: CatchPersonAvatarStack is a real person-avatar stack with overflow and veiled states, while HostTodayAvatarStack is a fixed decorative activity-dot preview for host today cards. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): HostTodayAvatarStack is a static decorative vignette (fixed dots, mock labels), not a data-driven stack. Naming candidate: HostTodayAvatarVignette.

K4 churn threshold: ChatsEmptyState delegates to CatchEmptyState but owns chat-specific named constructors and host-app copy switching; inlining would duplicate feature copy at call sites without simplifying the primitive.
K1 composition is not duplication: HostManageMetaRow lays out three weighted event metadata columns and constructs CatchMetaRow for the date, location, and capacity cells.

K5 concept mismatch: CatchTopBarTabBar is a PreferredSizeWidget wrapper for Material tabs under a top bar, while ProfileTabBar is a profile-specific segmented CatchOptionGroup rail with edit/preview/insights state. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): different control primitives (Material TabBar vs CatchOptionGroup bound to a TabController); ProfileTabBar is a future CatchTabRail variant candidate, not a merge.
K2 domain fork: the club and event detail bodies share broad detail-page shape but own different route state, hero app bars, sections, action callbacks, and domain-specific navigation.

K5 concept mismatch: EventAgendaSliverList groups event rows into a sliver agenda, while EventDetailHintList renders static event-detail hints in a hairline list with activity-colored dots. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): cross-concept structural coincidence; different design intents (per K5 read of names/roles against prior batch context).
K1 composition is not duplication: CatchDetailSliverSectionList is a detail-screen sliver adapter that applies detail padding and delegates its child layout to CatchSectionList.

No exact K/R match: CatchScreenBody is a generic padding/scroll shell, while PublicProfileScreenBody is a route-state dispatcher for loading/error/unavailable/ready profile states. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): name collision only — PublicProfileScreenBody is a route status switch (StateView), not a body primitive. Naming-pass candidate: PublicProfileStateView.

K5 concept mismatch: CatchPrivacyBadge is a core uppercase privacy token badge with transparent surface styling, while the Event Success PrivacyBadge maps a private audience enum to CatchBadge labels and icons. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): privacy vocabulary is a trust surface — one component. Extend CatchPrivacyBadgeKind with privateToYou/hostCanSee/catchPrivate (labels/icons from the companion widget); companion standardizes to the core mono-pill treatment (intentional visual change). Also fix raw size: 11 -> CatchIcon.micro in the core badge.

No exact K/R match: CatchFieldRow is a core row layout primitive, while ProfileFieldRow is a descriptor dispatcher across read-only, text, height, and enum inline editors. REVIEW (claude, 2026-07-03): K1: descriptor-driven dispatcher composing CatchField and profile editors.
K1 composition is not duplication: CreateEventPhotoPicker is a feature adapter that supplies event-photo labels and copy while delegating the picker mechanics to OrderedPhotoPicker.
